purpose: Per-glyph OCR drop (deletion) and corrupting-substitution rates for the base52g clean-52 candidate alphabet (the 52-glyph max-independent-set candidate from the confusion-ceiling analysis), with basemaxg's 43-glyph set as a calibration control (known thin-glyph drops: backtick, ;, |). Measured with tools/ocr_font_report.py's new per-glyph deletion stats (aligned diff via difflib.SequenceMatcher, not naive positional zip).

method.engine: tesseract 5.4.1 (source build), stock eng.traineddata (/root/gtrain32/tessdata_best), no fine-tuning

gate_thresholds.note: a glyph is drop-gated OUT if its deletion_rate OR corrupting-substitution rate exceeds epsilon on EITHER font

basemaxg43_control.source: basemaxg's shipped 43-glyph alphabet, used here purely as a calibration control (we know its thin glyphs drop)

predict_then_check.prediction: the drop-gated measurement should flag basemaxg's known-bad thin glyphs (backtick, semicolon, pipe) as high-drop/high-corruption
